hints.text: Autonomous region of Portugal → **Portuguese infrastructure** (poles, chevrons etc.)

shared_with.note: Same Portuguese infra on the Azores.
The Azores are less mountainous and more temperate.
shared_with.diff: Steep + semi-tropical → Madeira; rolling green pasture → Azores.

hints.text: Lots of **eucalyptus forests** — can look deceptively like mainland Portugal

shared_with.note: The mainland is full of eucalyptus too.
The Azores do NOT commonly have eucalypti.

hints.text: Flora: **African lilies** (long grassy leaves, round white/blue flower clusters on tall stems) very common; **hydrangeas** occasionally; **ferns** everywhere

shared_with.note: Hydrangeas are even MORE common on the Azores.
shared_with.diff: Hydrangea walls everywhere → Azores; lilies + steep terrain → Madeira.

hints.text: **Terrace farming** extensively (no flat land) — stone walls separate the plateaus
hints.uniq_note: Within the Portuguese world the dense stone-wall terraces scream Madeira.

hints.text: Houses white/grey/pastel (yellow, pink) w/ **red tile roofs in good condition**
Bus-stop signs in a unique **black/white/yellow** scheme; taxis **yellow w/ blue side stripe**
Small unique **stone bollards w/ red reflector** — cuboidal or cylindrical
Sometimes **yellow-and-black guardrails** on mountain roads; bridge railings often painted **dark green**; signposts often **striped** (also Azores)
These highway **speed-limit signs are unique to Madeira**
**Low clouds/fog** typical; **tunnels** very common in the mountains
Free-roaming **cows on high roads** + cow warning signs — Madeira's are **brown w/ white spots**

shared_with.note: Azorean cows are typically black and white.

hints.text: Weird low-quality **'shitcam'** coverage (official Ari): wide circular blur, gloomy image, often an **unblurred white car back** (good maps exclude it, but be aware)
hints.uniq_note: This camera + white car back is a Madeira signature when it does appear.

hints.text: **Banana plantations → south**; centre-west = elevated **plateau** (flat, misty, wind turbines); road east of the plateau = distinctive rocks + black-yellow guardrail; **eastern tip = arid**, nearly no vegetation

bullets: ER211: dark pavement + exposed brownish soil
Pico Areeiro: 2nd-highest peak (highest w/ coverage), island view + white radar station
Penha d'Águia: isolated elevated hill in the NE
Cristiano Ronaldo Airport (east): north end on numerous concrete pillars
Cascata dos Anjos (SW): 100-m waterfall cascading onto the road
Ribeira de Janela islets (NW): vertical jagged photographer spot
Porto Moniz (NW): dark sharp rock formations w/ an island behind
